title: Execute a Julia script
summary: Execute a Julia script via julia. Stdout is captured line-by-line as Progress events and written to run.log. Stderr is captured line-by-line as Warning events and written to stderr.log. A non-zero exit code produces Finished { success: false }. Use the 'project' field to activate a local Julia environment (Project.toml / Manifest.toml) so package imports resolve without requiring a globally installed environment.
description:
Runs a Julia script and streams its output back to the Open Choice protocol. Provide a path to a `.jl` file via `script`. Positional and named arguments can be forwarded to the script via `args` and `kwargs`. All output files (run.log, stderr.log) are written to `output_dir`.

The `project` field activates a local Julia environment by passing `--project=<path>` to the julia command, so package imports resolve against that environment's `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml`. The environment must already be instantiated (`Pkg.instantiate()`) before running.

The `trust_acknowledged` field must be set to `true`. This is an explicit safety gate: the plugin executes arbitrary Julia code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process.
usage:
[["julia-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "<path/to/script.jl>"
output_dir = "<dir>"
trust_acknowledged = true
# optional:
args = ["arg1", "arg2"]
kwargs = { key = "value" }
project = "."

arguments.name: script
arguments.description: Path to the .jl file to execute. Relative paths are resolved from the directory containing the .oce file. Absolute paths are used as-is.

arguments.name: args
arguments.description: Positional arguments appended to the julia command after the script path. Accessed in Julia via ARGS.

arguments.name: kwargs
arguments.description: Named arguments passed as --key value pairs after positional args. Accessed in Julia via ARGS or an arg-parser package such as ArgParse.jl.

arguments.name: project
arguments.description: Julia project directory to activate. Passed as --project=<path> before the script, so Julia loads the Project.toml and Manifest.toml in that directory. Relative paths are resolved from the .oce file directory. Common values: '.' to use the project co-located with the .oce file; a subdirectory name to point at a nested project; an absolute path for a shared environment. The project must already have been instantiated (Pkg.instantiate()) — this flag selects the environment but does not install missing packages.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory where run.log and stderr.log are written. Created if it does not exist.

arguments.name: trust_acknowledged
arguments.description: Must be true. Explicitly acknowledges that this plugin executes arbitrary Julia code with the same OS permissions as the Open Choice process, and that the script source is trusted.

details:
`run_script` is a thin execution wrapper: it invokes `julia` on the provided script, streams stdout line-by-line as Progress events, and writes all output to `output_dir/run.log`. Stderr lines are captured as Warning events and written to `output_dir/stderr.log`.

The `trust_acknowledged = true` field is required and intentional — it prevents accidental execution of untrusted code by requiring the caller to explicitly opt in.

Argument forwarding:
- `args` becomes positional arguments after the script path on the command line
- `kwargs` becomes `--key value` pairs following the positional args
In Julia, retrieve them with `ARGS`. For structured parsing, use `ArgParse.jl`.

The `project` field is the recommended way to use Julia packages: point it at a directory containing a `Project.toml` (typically the same directory as your script or the `.oce` file) to use that project's exact package versions. The project must already be instantiated.
value:
On success, `output_dir` contains:

- `run.log` — all stdout lines from the Julia process.
- `stderr.log` — all stderr lines from the Julia process.

The plugin emits `event.run.started`, per-line Progress events, and `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed` on non-zero exit).
notes: The `trust_acknowledged` field must be `true`; any other value (including omitting it) causes the command to reject immediately.
Non-zero exit codes from Julia produce `event.run.failed`; the log files are still written.
The `project` path must contain a valid Project.toml and Manifest.toml — run `Pkg.instantiate()` first if needed.
Julia's first-run JIT compilation can make initial execution slow; subsequent runs on the same DEPOT are faster.

see_also.description: R equivalent — run a .R file or inline R code.
Python equivalent — run a .py file and optionally use a virtualenv.

examples.title: Run a Julia script with the global environment
Run a script using a local Julia project environment
Pass kwargs to the script using ArgParse.jl
examples.body:
[["julia-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/export.jl"
project = "."
kwargs = { input = "./data/part_worths.csv", output = "./outputs/summary.csv" }
output_dir = "./runs/julia-export"
trust_acknowledged = true

examples.title: Run a script with positional args and a nested project
examples.body:
[["julia-wrapper::run_script"]]
script = "./analysis/simulate.jl"
project = "./analysis"
args = ["1000", "42"]
output_dir = "./runs/julia-simulate"
trust_acknowledged = true

fields.name: script
fields.description: Path to the .jl file to execute.

fields.name: args
fields.description: Positional args forwarded to the script via ARGS.

fields.name: kwargs
fields.description: Named args forwarded as --key value pairs.

fields.name: project
fields.description: Julia project dir to activate via --project. Must be pre-instantiated.

fields.name: trust_acknowledged
fields.description: Must be true. Safety gate for arbitrary code execution.
